strengths: Nearly 20 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and high-scale infrastructure (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby), comfortably exceeding the 15-year minimum
Currently building AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq — MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows — direct, present-day builder fluency in the AI focus area the role targets

Founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes and adopted by Green Map, plus a sustained record of conference talks and multi-day workshops — matching the entrepreneurial and 'inspire others through sharing insights' expectations
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and dedicated AI work is recent (2025–present) rather than a long-established, externally recognized AI track record
Career titles peak at Staff/Senior engineer; the resume shows no formal director-level scope or organization-wide strategy ownership
Cloud experience is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than cloud platform strategy at the scale the role advises on

key_factors.factor: Depth and breadth of engineering experience (20 years, full stack to low-latency infrastructure)
Current hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work
Founder experience and public teaching/speaking record

response.justification: The candidate clears the role's core bar — 15+ years of software and infrastructure engineering — with room to spare, and his experience spans exactly the range this role synthesizes: low-latency systems at massive scale, cloud-deployed product engineering, and, most relevantly, current hands-on work building AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks. His decade-long founder role at GISCollective demonstrates the entrepreneurial initiative the preferred qualifications call for, and his consistent record of conference talks, workshops, and courses maps directly onto the responsibility to advise partners and inspire others through shared insight. While his AI specialization is recent and his titles have been IC-level, the combination of proven builder fluency, breadth across the stack, and demonstrated thought-sharing makes him a credible, high-upside fit for a director-level IC role focused on identifying and prototyping AI-driven differentiation.
